name: Creole Fish
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Green Bell Pepper, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven; lightly sauté onions, celery, green bell pepper, and garlic until soft (do not brown).
2. Add tomatoes, tomato puree, bay leaf, thyme, black pepper, sugar, salt, Worcestershire sauce, and Tabasco sauce.
3. Stir well; bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer at least 1 hour.
4. Correct seasoning as needed.
5. Blend cornstarch into water; stir briskly into the sauce and cook until translucent and thick.
6. Prepare fish as desired: Steam or poach fillets; bake (in sauce of lemon juice and melted butter) in a preheated 375°F oven for 20 to 30 minutes; deep-fry breaded fillets. Serve with warm sauce, allowing 1/3 to 1/2 cup per serving.
7. NOTE: Unbreaded fish fillets may be baked directly in the sauce, too. Thaw fish if frozen; pat dry and place in well-oiled shallow baking dishes. Cover with sauce; bake in a preheated 375°F oven for 25 to 30 minutes, or until fish flakes easily.
